summary: As societies progress, there’s an observable shift towards managing more chronic conditions rather than infections; this process matches what epidemiologists call "Epidemiological Transition." To summarize further would be redundant given these clear defining factors have been highlighted already. Agent1 concludes based on extensive documentation supporting the concept of Epidemiological Transition which accurately describes the described scenario involving shifts between types of prevalent causes of death and illness over time due to societal advancement. Key aspects include moving away from communicable/infectious illnesses toward primarily addressing non-infectious/chronic ailments like those resulting from modern lifestyles or environmental exposures instead.
